title: Modern ships of war
author: Reed, Edward J. (Edward James), Sir
summary: Modern Ships of War, by Sir Edward J. Reed and Rear-Admiral Edward Simpson with supplementary notes by J. D. J. Kelley, surveys late-nineteenth-century naval development. It traces the rapid evolution from wooden sail fleets to iron-clad and steel armored vessels, analyzes national navies (British, French, Italian, Russian, German, Austrian, Turkish, and American), and details design, armament, artillery, and torpedo and submarine warfare. Illustrated plans and ship sections accompany discussions of specific ships, gun types, and rapid-fire ordnance. Appendices address submarine and torpedo technology, reserve naval forces, forced draft, ship types, and gun ranges, forming a technical yet accessible reference guide.

characters.name: Sir Edward J. Reed
characters.personality: authoritative, experienced naval constructor and analyst
characters.lifespan: Presented as the principal author and expert; survives as the book's guiding authority

characters.name: Edward Simpson
characters.personality: practical, professional naval officer and analyst
characters.lifespan: Co-author and contributor from U.S. naval perspective; remains as a technical authority in the work

characters.name: J. D. Jerrold Kelley
characters.lifespan: Contributor of supplementary chapters and notes; appears as augmenting the main text

characters.name: J. O. Davidson
characters.lifespan: Credited for the drawing of the Battle of Trafalgar frontispiece; included as artist only
